pattern: With at most 26 distinct letters and 8 keys, assign each letter to a key slot; greedy assignment by frequency minimises total pushes since slots 1-8 cost 1 push, 9-16 cost 2, etc.
hints: There are 8 keys (2-9); the first 8 letters assigned (one per key) cost 1 push each, the next 8 cost 2 pushes each, and so on.
The word contains only distinct letters, so each letter appears exactly once; the total cost equals the position-based slot cost summed over all letters.
Sort frequencies (here all 1) and greedily fill slots — but since all counts are 1 here, any assignment gives cost = sum of floor((i)/8)+1 for i in 0..len-1.

edge_inputs.description: letters not in alphabetical order should not matter

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import random
    letters = list('abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz')
    rng.shuffle(letters)
    n = rng.randint(1, 26)
    return [''.join(letters[:n])]
